data.title: Hims & Hers down as FDA advisors snub first peptide
data.description:
Shares of Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) dropped more than 11% on Friday after an FDA advisory panel ​reviewing whether to recommend loosening restrictions on a group of popular peptides indicated its first negative vote.

While six members of the FDA’s pharmacy compounding advisory committee voted in favor of emideltide, a peptide touted for its benefits in opioid withdrawal and chronic insomnia, seven voted against, and one member abstained.

Emideltide was among 14 peptides that the FDA proposed to include on its 503A Bulks List, which compounding pharmacies are allowed to use in the production of customized medications for their clients.

On Thursday, the committee voted in favor of four additional peptides, and the meeting continued on Friday with voting scheduled for emideltide, epitalon, and semax. Hims & Hers (HIMS) is a leading compounding pharmacy operator, having acquired a peptide facility in California last year.

data.title: U.S. stocks rise as oil prices retreat from $100 level
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages aimed to go higher on Friday as oil prices decreased. Nonetheless, the indexes are on track for another losing week.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.4%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.5%.

Week-to-date, the S&P 500 is on track for its second weekly loss, the longest since a five-week stretch in March.

President Donald Trump is looking to resume major military operations in Iran, according to reports. The president told Axios he was deciding whether to authorize “a massive attack, bigger than ever before” but had not reached a final decision.

Trump also said China and Russia had assured him they would not supply weapons to Iran.

Oil prices rose above $100 per barrel on Thursday after Houthi rebels reportedly struck two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea.

However, the oil markets have subsided. Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were higher at $88, while Brent (CO1:COM) was $96 per barrel.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropped 3 basis points to 4.67%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was 4 basis points lower at 4.32%.

In other news, the Trump administration imposed new levies on 60 trading partners, including Canada and Mexico, as well as Japan and the European Union, collecting duties of between 10% and 12.5%.

Lastly, on the economic calendar, the U.S. PMI Composite rose to 53.6 in July, vs. 54.2 consensus, preliminary data released by S&P Global showed.

The June U.S. new home sales topped consensus, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

data.title: Flexible Finance applies for Utah-chartered industrial bank
data.description:
Fintech Flexible Finance on Friday said it has applied to the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation and the Utah Department of Financial Institutions to charter Flex Bank.

The proposed Salt Lake City-headquartered bank will operate nationally exclusively through digital channels.

The Utah state-chartered industrial bank is expected to issue the FDIC-insured institution's core credit products, including Flex Rent.

"A bank charter allows us to build directly on a foundation of federal deposit insurance and full state and federal bank regulatory oversight, strengthening the products millions of renters already rely on," said CEO Shragie Lichtenstein.

The news comes after the money transfer company Wise Group's (WSE) application to become a national trust bank in the U.S. was rejected by the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency.

Earlier this month, Klarna (KLAR) submitted an application to the Utah Department of Financial Institutions and the FDIC to establish Klarna Bank USA.

In May, Senator Elizabeth Warren raised concerns over granting national trust charters to companies that do not qualify under the National Bank Act.

Warren sent a letter to the chief officer of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Jonathan Gould, questioning his decision.

data.title: Progressive raised to Equalweight at Morgan Stanley on steady quarterly trends
data.description:
Progressive (PGR) stock rose 3.0% in Friday midday trading after Morgan Stanley upgraded the P&C insurer to Equalweight, citing “relatively steady” trends and a reset on its valuation.

In the past month, Progressive (PGR) stock slipped 1.2%, compared with a 1.3% increase for the S&P 500 during the same period.

"While the monthly results have been volatile to the upside and downside, the overall quarterly results indicate relatively steady trends," analyst Bob Jian Huang wrote in a note to clients. Furthermore, its recent share price decline, the stock now represents a "somewhat fair valuation."

"Progressive continues to see a notably better than market level of premium growth (albeit slowing)," he wrote. "Combining auto growth and underwriting margins, we would note that Progressive continues to outperform Allstate & Travelers. We believe growth is likely to remain structurally better at Progressive given the flexibility between bundled and mono-line auto businesses." 

The Equalweight rating aligns with the SA Quant rating and average Wall Street rating of Hold and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: Tenet Healthcare surges on Q2 earnings beat, lifts full-year guidance
data.description:
Tenet Healthcare (THC) surged 20% on Friday after the hospital operator reported second-quarter results that comfortably exceeded Wall Street expectations and raised its full-year 2026 guidance across key financial metrics.

For the quarter ended June 30, the company reported adjusted EPS of $6.12, beating analysts' estimates of $4.26, while revenue rose 6.6% Y/Y to $5.62B, ahead of the $5.44B consensus. Growth was driven by strong performance across both operating segments, with ambulatory net operating revenue increasing 9.3% to $1.38B and hospital revenue rising 6% to $4.20B.

Profitability also improved, as the hospital adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 18.0% from 15.6% a year earlier despite payer mix headwinds. Overall, adjusted EBITDA increased to $1.30B from $1.12B, supported by strong same-facility revenue growth and disciplined expense management.

Reflecting the strong quarter, Tenet raised its full-year 2026 outlook. The company now expects adjusted EBITDA of $4.83B-$5.03B, up from the previous range of $4.48B-$4.78B. It also increased its adjusted free cash flow forecast to $2.73B-$3.03B from $2.50B-$2.80B, adjusted EPS guidance to $20.30-$21.69 (consensus estimate: $18.50) from $16.38-$18.68, and net operating revenue guidance to $21.9B-$22.5B ($22.03B estimate) from $21.5B-$22.3B.

Major rivals also gained. HCA Healthcare (HCA) 2.4%, Universal Health Services (UHS) +4.6%, and Community Health Systems (CYH) +1.9% were among the notable gainers.

data.title: Trump's tariff plan eases retailers' worst-case fears
data.description:
Clothing manufacturers that rely on overseas labor are breathing a sigh of relief as President Trump unveiled import tariffs that were largely in line with expectations.

To replace tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ((IEEPA)) that expired at 12:01 am on July 24, the Administration slapped 60 countries with tariffs of 10% to 12.5% for their failure to prevent and enforce a ban on forced labor.

“President Trump recognizes that decades of moral suasion have not eradicated forced labor from global supply chains. The United States has had a forced labor import ban for nearly a century and rigorously enforces it; it’s well past time for our trading partners to do the same,” said U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

The ruling will impose a 10% import tariff on goods—excluding oil, gas, fertilizer, and certain food items—from many Central and South American countries, as well as those responsible for much of the production of merchandise from large U.S. businesses including Nike (NKE), PVH (PVH), Levi Strauss (LEVI), and Lululemon (LULU).

A tariff of 10% or 12.5% net of the Most-Favored-Nation rate has been imposed on goods from the EU, Taiwan, Japan, Korea, and Switzerland, while 12.5% applies to merchandise imported from all other investigated economies. 

To mitigate the impact on their bottom line from the higher cost of imports, companies like Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF), Target (TGT), Walmart (WMT), and Best Buy (BBY) raised prices while still bracing for the full impact from U.S. trade policy on year-end results. Many located alternative sources but absorbed higher supply chain costs, such as RH (RH) which realized an increased cost of $75M in tariff-related resourcing.

Crucially, the majority of FY26 projections incorporated 10% to 12% tariffs for the remainder of the year once the initial IEEPA tariffs expired.

“Tariffs remain a dynamic cost headwind that we expect to continue looking forward,” Nike CFO Matthew Friend said on the company’s fiscal fourth quarter earnings call.

For Levi Strauss (LEVI) the company assumed a worst-case scenario for FY26 guidance modeling 30% tariffs on Chinese imports and 20% for the rest-of-world. With the new rates set at 10% to 12.5% for China and 10% for the rest-of-world, the improved outlook was reflected in a 2% gain for Levi Strauss (LEVI) shares on Friday.

With the tariff rates now largely defined, retailers and apparel companies have greater visibility into import costs for the remainder of the year, allowing them to better plan pricing, sourcing, and earnings expectations.

Accordingly, the Consumer Staples and Consumer Discretionary ETFs are both higher Friday.

data.title: Canadian Solar opens flagship solar cell factory in Indiana, boosting U.S. presence
data.description:
Canadian Solar (CSIQ) said Friday it has opened the first phase of its flagship PV cell manufacturing facility at the River Ridge Commerce Center in Jeffersonville, Indiana, seeking to help fill a major gap in U.S. solar ​manufacturing by producing a key component that is largely made in Asia.

The ​nearly $1B investment by Canadian Solar's (CSIQ) U.S. manufacturing arm, CS PowerTech, is ​expected to produce 6 GW/year of cells and employ ⁠more than 1,200 workers once it reaches full production by early next year.

The company said the Jeffersonville PVCells plant is the first U.S. PV cell facility designed to produce heterojunction bifacial N-type solar cells, which it said offers better performance than conventional technologies and ‌has ⁠more room for future improvements.

Output from ⁠the Jeffersonville plant will be used by Canadian Solar's module factory in Mesquite, Texas, which is being expanded to ​10 GW of annual capacity.

The new plant "will directly reduce our reliance ​on imported ⁠cells," although the company will still depend on some imports, Canadian Solar (CSIQ) CEO Colin Parkin told Reuters.

data.title: ClearBridge ESG Strategy adds SpaceX, AMD, and Micron; exits Roblox, AbbVie
data.description:
• ClearBridge Appreciation ESG Strategy underperformed the S&P 500 Index in Q2 2026, which returned 15.2% during the quarter, as the strategy’s positioning lagged the technology-led market rally.
  • During the quarter, the strategy initiated new positions in SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX), Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU), Cisco Systems (NASDAQ: CSCO), Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD), Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN), and Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ: FANG).
  • The strategy exited its holdings in Roblox (NYSE: RBLX), Boston Scientific (NYSE: BSX), AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV), and Solstice Advanced Materials (NASDAQ: SOLS) in Q2 2026.
  • Source.

data.title: LeBron James signs with the Sixers, sparking fresh interest in gaming and betting stocks
data.description:
Philadelphia has landed one of the NBA’s biggest names. LeBron James is joining the 76ers on a two-year, $8 million contract that includes a player option, according to the star’s announcement. The move immediately elevates championship expectations in a city that has not won an NBA title since 1983.

Beyond the basketball implications, the signing is expected to drive heightened engagement across the sports betting and gaming sector. As one of the league’s most recognizable athletes joins a major market franchise, wagering volume and related media activity are likely to rise. 

Operators including Penn Entertainment (PENN), MGM Resorts International (MGM), Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), and DraftKings (DKNG) stand to benefit from the increased interest. Investors tracking the broader theme can also monitor gaming and gambling ETFs such as the Roundhill Sports Betting & iGaming ETF (BETZ) and Pacer BlueStar Digital Entertainment ETF (ODDS) for potential secondary effects. 

The combination of star power, a competitive Eastern Conference roster, and Philadelphia’s passionate fan base creates a possible catalyst for near-term activity in the sports gaming space. Still, any title path for the Sixers would require navigating through the defending champion New York Knicks—and Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. (MSGS), which has already won big this year, with shares surging 54.8% in 2026.
